api/files.title: Files API
api/files.description: The Files API provides a single endpoint for listing the available .fe simulation datafiles on the server. It is used by the FilePane component to populate the file explorer.

api/jobs.title: Jobs API
api/jobs.description: The Jobs API manages asynchronous iteration jobs created by POST /api/sessions//iterate. A job represents a batch gradient-descent run that executes in the background while the server continues accepting other requests.

api/sessions.title: Sessions API
api/sessions.description: Sessions represent a loaded .fe file in the Surface Evolver engine. Because the engine is a global singleton, only one session's surface data can be active at a time (tracked by activesessionid in semanager). However, multiple SessionState records can coexist in the in-memory store.

api/simulation.title: Simulation API
api/simulation.description: The simulation routes operate on an existing session and provide the core interaction with the SE engine: running commands, iterating the surface, and retrieving mesh geometry. All routes are prefixed with /api/sessions/.

api/websocket.title: WebSocket API
api/websocket.description: The WebSocket endpoint pushes real-time iteration progress to connected clients. It uses a simple per-session connection registry — any number of clients can connect to a session's progress stream simultaneously.

architecture/data-flow.title: Data Flow
architecture/data-flow.description: This document traces how data moves through the system for each major user action: loading a file, running a command, starting an iteration job, and fetching mesh geometry.

architecture/dependencies.title: External Dependencies
architecture/dependencies.description: This document lists every significant external library and service used by the project, explains the role each plays, and notes the version constraints in effect.

architecture/overview.title: Architecture Overview
architecture/overview.description: This document describes the high-level system design of the Surface Evolver web interface. The system translates HTTP requests from a browser into calls to the original Surface Evolver C engine and streams the resulting mesh data back to the frontend for 3D visualization.

components/CliPane.title: CliPane Component
components/CliPane.description: CliPane is the terminal-like pane in the center of the main layout. It displays simulation statistics at the top, a scrollable output log in the middle, and a command input line at the bottom. Users can type SE commands directly and see the engine's output in real time.

components/FilePane.title: FilePane Component
components/FilePane.description: FilePane is the file explorer panel on the left side of the main layout. It lists available .fe simulation datafiles fetched from the backend, and creates a session when the user selects a file.

components/store-and-hooks.title: Zustand Store and Custom Hooks
components/store-and-hooks.description: The frontend uses two state management mechanisms in parallel: Zustand for client-side UI state and TanStack React Query for server-side cached data. This document covers both the Zustand store and the two custom hooks that connect the store to the backend.

components/ViewerPane.title: ViewerPane Component
components/ViewerPane.description: ViewerPane is the 3D WebGL visualization pane on the right side of the main layout. It renders the current triangulated mesh from the Surface Evolver engine using Three.js via react-three-fiber, with orbit controls for interactive rotation, pan, and zoom.

contributing/code-style.title: Code Style
contributing/code-style.description: This document describes the coding conventions used in each layer of the project. Following these conventions ensures consistency and makes code review easier.

contributing/workflow.title: Development Workflow
contributing/workflow.description: This document describes the recommended workflow for contributing to the Surface Evolver project, including branch strategy, testing requirements, and deployment steps.

gui-dev/implementation.title: Surface Evolver GUI — Implementation Roadmap
gui-dev/implementation.description: Agile sprints, 2 weeks each. Each sprint ships working, tested software. Stories are sized S / M / L (roughly ½ day / 1 day / 2–3 days).

gui-dev/test-infra.title: Surface Evolver GUI — Testing Infrastructure
gui-dev/test-infra.description: End-to-end testing strategy covering all layers of the stack. Every sprint in implementation.md ships with tests that meet the coverage targets below.

modules/backend-core.title: Backend Core
modules/backend-core.description: The backend core (backend/app/core/) contains three modules that together manage the Surface Evolver engine singleton, asynchronous job execution, and session persistence.

modules/backend-routers.title: Backend Routers
modules/backend-routers.description: The FastAPI application registers four HTTP routers and one WebSocket router. All routers are mounted in backend/app/main.py with no common prefix (each router declares its own prefix).

modules/c-api.title: C API Facade
modules/c-api.description: The C API facade (bindings/c/seapi.h and seapi.c) provides a minimal, stable ABI boundary between the Surface Evolver C engine and external callers (Python via CFFI, or any other FFI). It exposes exactly 17 functions covering the full lifecycle from initialization through mesh geometry extraction.

modules/c-engine.title: C Engine
modules/c-engine.description: The Surface Evolver C engine (src/) is the numerical core of the system. It implements a mesh-based variational method for minimizing surface energy functionals with constraints. This document gives an overview of the source organization, key subsystems, and how they connect to the C API facade.

modules/fe-datafiles.title: .fe Simulation Datafiles
modules/fe-datafiles.description: The fe/ directory contains the bundled Surface Evolver datafiles (.fe extension) that ship with the project. These files define the initial mesh geometry, constraints, energy functionals, and simulation parameters for various classic surface-energy problems.

modules/python-binding.title: Python Binding
modules/python-binding.description: bindings/python/se.py provides a Pythonic wrapper around libse.so. It uses CFFI in ABI mode — meaning it loads the precompiled shared library at runtime without any compilation step. The module exposes a single class SurfaceEvolver and an exception class SEError.

README.title: Surface Evolver — Project Overview
README.description: Surface Evolver is a web-based interactive interface for Ken Brakke's Surface Evolver C simulation engine. It wraps the original C binary in a REST API and a modern browser UI so that surface-energy minimization simulations can be run, visualized, and controlled interactively — without requiring any local C toolchain or display server.

setup/deploy-railway.title: Deploying on Railway
setup/deploy-railway.description: This guide deploys the app as two Railway services (backend + frontend) within a single Railway project. Services communicate over Railway's private network — no public URL hop needed for API proxying.

setup/environment-variables.title: Environment Variables
setup/environment-variables.description: All backend configuration is managed through backend/app/config.py using pydantic-settings. Variables can be set via the shell environment or a .env file in the backend working directory.

setup/installation.title: Installation
setup/installation.description: This document covers how to get the project building from source. Two paths are supported: Docker (recommended for the full stack) and native (required for C development or debugging the engine).

setup/running-locally.title: Running Locally
setup/running-locally.description: This document covers how to run and debug the project in a local development environment. It assumes you have already completed the Installation steps.
